---
title: "MAST_Visium_Analysis"
author: "Alos Diallo"
date: "2026-01-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(Matrix)
  library(MAST)
  library(dplyr)
  library(readr)
  library(tibble)
})
```

```{r}
# 0. Load the bridge library
library(SingleCellExperiment)

# --- Step 1: Load Data (Same as before) ---
base_path <- "/Users/adiallo/Desktop/Thesis/Data_Documents/All_Data/Visium_samples/Visium_Data/samples/"

counts <- readMM(paste0(base_path, "mast_counts.mtx"))
metadata <- read_csv(paste0(base_path, "mast_metadata.csv"))
gene_info <- read_csv(paste0(base_path, "mast_gene_info.csv"))

# --- Step 2: Clean Metadata ---
metadata_df <- as.data.frame(metadata) %>% column_to_rownames(var = "...1")
gene_info_df <- as.data.frame(gene_info) %>% column_to_rownames(var = "...1")

# --- Step 3: Create a SingleCellExperiment ---
# This is the modern way to pass sparse data to MAST
# We use 'counts' directly as it is already a sparse Matrix object
sce <- SingleCellExperiment(
    assays = list(counts = as(counts, "CsparseMatrix")),
    colData = metadata_df,
    rowData = gene_info_df
)

# --- Step 1: Ensure Log Transformation is Sparse ---
# Use 'logcounts' as the assay name, as MAST looks for this by default
assay(sce, "logcounts") <- log2(assay(sce, "counts") + 1)

# --- Step 2: Convert to SingleCellAssay ---
sca <- as(sce, "SingleCellAssay")

# --- Step 3: Handle the missing IDs using Modern Syntax ---
# rowData() replaces fData()
# colData() replaces cData()
rowData(sca)$primerid <- rownames(sca)
colData(sca)$wellKey <- colnames(sca)

# --- Step 4: Verify Factor Levels ---
# Ensure the model knows which group is the baseline
colData(sca)$region <- factor(colData(sca)$region, levels = c("Center Tumor (CT)", "Invasive Margin (IM)"))
colData(sca)$mets_status <- factor(colData(sca)$mets_status, levels = c("No", "LN", "Distant"))
colData(sca)$patient_id <- as.factor(colData(sca)$patient_id)

# --- Step 5: Clean Memory ---
rm(sce)
gc()

# Check the object
print(sca)
```
```{r}
# 1. Clear memory again
gc()

# 2. Run the additive model sequentially
# It is better to wait 2 hours for a result than to crash 5 times in 10 minutes.
zlm_additive <- zlm(~ region + mets_status + DC2_abundance + QC + MLH1 + patient_id, 
                    sca, 
                    parallel = FALSE)

# 3. Save as soon as it's done
saveRDS(zlm_additive, "zlm_additive_final.rds")
```
